name: Caloric Restriction Mimetics Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Caloric Restriction Mimetics need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair checked the Mimio human biomarker RCT, its registry, related fasting-metabolite and supplement dose-escalation sources, acarbose human aging registry limitations, FMD/longevity-diet trial-watch records, and recent review/primary-source triangulation. The Mimio RCT is relevant to the taxonomy as a non-dietary fasting-mimetic supplement, but it remains small, sponsor-funded, 8 weeks long, biomarker and subjective-outcome weighted, and without functional, disease, disability, multimorbidity, mortality, or reported biological-age results. Acarbose human registry records remain limiting context rather than human aging efficacy evidence. FMD/longevity-diet registries remain no-results or trial-watch context. A recent acarbose cognition preprint adds animal healthspan context but is not peer-reviewed and does not change the current support map. The repaired assessment can move from thin to adequate coverage for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal claim while returning the track to ordinary surveillance.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repaired sources clarify source completeness and taxonomy boundaries but do not change the public stage, momentum, confidence, blockers, best-current-signal language, or rating-change criteria. No checked source adds replicated human functional or durable disease-relevant evidence.

search_log.summary: Confirmed the Mimio RCT as a February 20, 2026 Scientific Reports article testing an 8-week daily supplement in overweight older adults with elevated HbA1c. The publication reports hunger/satiety, digestive, lipid, oxidized LDL, and fasting-glucose signals, no significant cognitive or quality-of-life differences, and no adverse-event excess; the registry remains completed with no posted results.

Checked related Mimio-formulation and fasting-metabolite sources. PMID 39549554 is a 5-person dose-escalation study showing supplement bioavailability and ex vivo anti-inflammatory/antioxidant signals; PMID 36811567 is a 20-person fasting-metabolite pilot with C. elegans lifespan assays. They support mechanism context but not human aging efficacy.

Rechecked acarbose human aging registries. NCT02953093 is terminated with posted but non-analyzable or unreported primary and secondary endpoints; NCT02865499 is a very small uncontrolled microbiome-only study. Neither supplies human aging efficacy evidence.

Rechecked FMD, longevity-diet, and very-low-calorie-diet trial-watch records. The records remain no-results, unknown-status, recruiting, or completed-without-posted-results context and do not change public claims.

search_log.query: Recent review triangulation for CR mimetics, FMD, acarbose, spermidine, nicotinamide, palmitoylethanolamide, oleoylethanolamide, glucose-spike modulation, aging, longevity, lifespan, healthspan, biological age, frailty, and older adults, 2024-2026
search_log.summary: Recent review search returned broad aging therapeutics, FMD, acarbose, polyamine/autophagy, and spermidine-food review context. PMID 38966557, PMID 38287649, PMID 42225652, PMID 42086115, and PMID 42235230 did not reveal a missing primary anchor that changes the current public support map.

search_log.query: Recent primary and preprint triangulation for FMD, acarbose, ProLon, caloric-restriction mimetics, aging, longevity, lifespan, healthspan, biomarkers, and biological age, 2024-2026
search_log.summary: Returned adjacent records including PMID 42094534, PMID 42081078, and PMID 42060951. PMID 42094534 is a relevant acarbose normal-aging cognition preprint but not peer-reviewed; other close hits were mouse islet or oncology feasibility context rather than public support-map changes.

excluded_sources.title: A novel fasting mimetic (Mimio) creates fasting-like benefits to hunger control, oxidative stress, and cardiometabolic health in humans
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant non-dietary fasting-mimetic supplement RCT, but small, sponsor-funded, 8 weeks long, with 42 evaluable participants for subjective outcomes and 33 for blood markers. It reports cardiometabolic biomarkers rather than aging functional, disease, disability, multimorbidity, mortality, or reported biological-age endpoints.

excluded_sources.title: Observational Metabolic Health Study
excluded_sources.reason: Completed Mimio registry with no posted results; useful registry provenance for PMID 41720867 but not a separate public result.

excluded_sources.title: Absorption, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective impacts of a novel fasting mimetic containing spermidine, nicotinamide, palmitoylethanolamide, and oleoylethanolamide
excluded_sources.reason: Five-person dose-escalation study in healthy young adult men showing bioavailability and ex vivo plasma anti-inflammatory/antioxidant signals. Mechanistic support only, not aging efficacy.

excluded_sources.title: Absorption and Digestion Kinetics of Human Metabolites
excluded_sources.reason: Completed n = 5 registry record for the dose-escalation source, without posted registry results.

excluded_sources.title: Human fasting modulates macrophage function and upregulates multiple bioactive metabolites that extend lifespan in Caenorhabditis elegans
excluded_sources.reason: Mechanistic fasting-metabolite source and C. elegans lifespan assay. It supports the origin of the Mimio formulation but does not test a human CR-mimetic intervention for aging outcomes.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of Prolonged Fasting on Microbiome and HDL
excluded_sources.reason: Completed fasting-metabolite pilot registry with no posted results; article evidence is mechanistic context only.

excluded_sources.title: Study of Acarbose in Longevity
excluded_sources.reason: Terminated human acarbose crossover study with posted but non-informative tissue gene-expression, serum miRNA, and fecal microbiome outcome entries.

excluded_sources.title: Acarbose Anti-aging Effects in Geriatric Subjects
excluded_sources.reason: Very small uncontrolled acarbose microbiome pilot with no controlled aging biomarker, functional, disease, disability, multimorbidity, or mortality endpoint.

excluded_sources.title: Fasting-mimicking Diet and Longevity Diet, Body Composition and Aging
excluded_sources.reason: Unknown-status FMD/longevity-diet randomized registry with no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Longevity Diet and the Fasting-mimicking Diet
excluded_sources.reason: Completed retrospective longevity-diet/FMD registry with no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Follow up Study of Varapodio Trial: Effect of Longevity and Fasting Mimicking Diet on Risk Factors Age Correlated and Biomarkers of Aging
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting FMD/longevity-diet follow-up record with no posted results and expected completion in 2027.

excluded_sources.title: Fasting Mimicking Diet and Autophagy
excluded_sources.reason: Unknown-status autophagy-marker registry with no posted results; already represented by the published FMD autophagy source in the public map.

excluded_sources.title: Very Low-calorie Diet & Cardiometabolic Health
excluded_sources.reason: Completed very-low-calorie diet/autophagy marker registry with no posted results and no current public update.

excluded_sources.title: Acarbose improves cognitive function in a mouse model of normal aging but not Alzheimer's disease
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant acarbose animal-healthspan preprint, but it is not peer-reviewed and does not change the already published acarbose animal lifespan/healthspan boundary.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of Intermittent Fasting-Mimicking Diet on Pancreatic Islet Plasticity
excluded_sources.reason: Mouse pancreatic-islet plasticity paper, not a human aging, function, disease-burden, or longevity result.

excluded_sources.title: Brief Report: A Pilot Study Evaluating the Feasibility, Safety, and Compliance With a Fasting-Mimicking Diet in Patients With Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
excluded_sources.reason: Oncology feasibility/compliance context, not a CR-mimetic healthy-aging or LEV-relevant outcome update.

excluded_sources.title: Repurposing effect of cardiovascular-metabolic drug to increase lifespan
excluded_sources.reason: Systematic review reinforces acarbose animal-lifespan and clinical-trial-progress context, but does not add human acarbose aging efficacy.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of the periodic fasting-mimicking diet on health, lifespan, and multiple diseases
excluded_sources.reason: Narrative FMD review supports the existing FMD biomarker and trial-watch boundary without adding a new primary anchor.

excluded_sources.title: Insights into the therapeutic strategies for aging and aging-associated diseases
excluded_sources.reason: Broad aging therapeutics review, not a new primary CR-mimetic source.

excluded_sources.title: Polyamines and autophagy as a dynamic regulatory network in skeletal muscle regeneration and aging
excluded_sources.reason: Polyamine/autophagy review context for spermidine biology, not a Mimio or CR-mimetic human aging result.

excluded_sources.title: Spermidine in food and health
excluded_sources.reason: Spermidine review context, not a track-level public update for defined CR mimetics.

notes: Coverage repair was selected from the generated triage queue after the June 5 surveillance pass recommended source-completeness repair.

PMID 41720867 is relevant to the track taxonomy but not strong enough to stage as a public outlook update because it is a small short-duration biomarker and subjective-outcome RCT with sponsor funding and no durable aging endpoint.
The Mimio registry lists biological-age testing among secondary outcomes, but the checked publication and registry do not provide posted biological-age results.

PMID 42094534 should be watched for peer-reviewed publication because it may add acarbose animal-healthspan support, but the current public acarbose animal boundary is already represented by peer-reviewed lifespan and physical-function sources.
No new intervention record was created for Mimio because the repaired evidence does not yet justify a public support-map update.
next_actions: Resume ordinary surveillance for caloric restriction mimetics.
Watch for posted results or peer-reviewed publications from NCT06790407, NCT05698654, NCT07065955, NCT07255300, NCT06115551, NCT06560996, and PMID 42094534.
Stage a public candidate bundle only if future Mimio, FMD, acarbose, or other CR-mimetic evidence changes the stage, support map, blockers, confidence, or rating-change criteria.
